channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - wccftech.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for wccftech.com

item.title: Xbox Game Pass Games Hub – Every Game Available in Xbox Game Pass
item.content:encoded:
Once considered 'the best deal in gaming,' Microsoft's subscription service, Xbox Game Pass, is used by millions of players each day due to its truly vast library of titles you can play on your PC, Xbox One, Xbox Series X/S, or any device with the Xbox Game Pass app and a WiFi connection through Cloud Streaming.

This hub will take you through everything you need to know about with regards to Game Pass, including the current pricing and tier structure, and every game currently available in the service, broken down by which games are available for players at the Essential, Premium, and Ultimate tiers.

Table of Contents

  • Game Pass Tiers (Pricing, Features)
    • Game Pass Essential - $9.99/month
    • Game Pass Premium - $14.99/month
    • Game Pass Ultimate - $29.99/month
    • PC Game Pass - $16.49/month
  • Xbox Game Pass Games (by Tier)
    • Xbox Game Pass Essential Games (A-Z)
    • Xbox Game Pass Premium Games (A-Z)
    • Xbox Game Pass Ultimate (A-Z)

Game Pass Tiers (Pricing, Features)

NOTE: All prices are in USD, pricing may vary based on your region. We will continue to update this list as new games are added to the service and games are removed.

Game Pass Essential - $9.99/month

Starting with the lowest tier, Game Pass Essential is your foot in the door with spending as little as possible on the subscription. At just $9.99/month, a subscription to Xbox Game Pass Essential gets you:

  • A library of 50+ games you can access through your PC, console, or Cloud streaming
  • Online multiplayer on consoles
  • In-game benefits for select titles
  • Up to $25 a year can be earned through the Microsoft Rewards program

One thing to note about the Essential tier and its 'library of 50+ games,' which Microsoft touts on its website, is that several of the titles it includes as part of the library you get with Game Pass Essential are free-to-play games.

If you're a console player, technically, these games would be unavailable to you without a Game Pass Essential subscription, because they all require online multiplayer, something that is paywalled on consoles - but not on PC. It's also worth noting that while you can stream games through Xbox Cloud gaming wherever you want, for as long as you want, Essential subscribers get the worst possible experience with the longest wait times. Every rung up the subscription ladder you go gets you better streaming capabilities.

Game Pass Premium - $14.99/month

Game Pass Premium is one rung above Essential, and is technically the middle tier. For an extra $5 per month at $14.99/month, a subscription to Xbox Game Pass Premium gets you:

  • 200+ games you can access through your PC, console, or Cloud streaming
  • Brand new Xbox-published titles added to the Game Pass Premium games library within one year of their initial release date
  • Online multiplayer on consoles
  • In-game benefits for select titles
  • Up to $50 a year can be earned through the Microsoft Rewards program

If the current pricing structure wasn't a massive increase compared to what we had for years, Game Pass Premium might actually feel like a good deal.

Game Pass Ultimate - $29.99/month

Game Pass Ultimate is the most expensive, top-tier in Microsoft's subscription service. This is for all the marbles, all the bells and whistles you could want, but most importantly, for the day-one releases of brand new Xbox published games.

For double the price of Game Pass Premium, a $29.99/month subscription to Xbox Game Pass Ultimate gets you:

  • 400+ games you can access through your PC, console, or Cloud streaming
  • 75+ brand new games a year on day one at launch, including all Xbox-published games
  • The library of games included in Ubisoft+ Classics and EA Play
  • Online multiplayer on consoles
  • In-game benefits for select titles
  • Up to $100 a year can be earned through the Microsoft Rewards program

PC Game Pass - $16.49/month

Last but most certainly not least, because it is arguably the best deal among all of the Game Pass tiers, is PC Game Pass. Whether you have an Xbox console or not, if you have a gaming-capable PC and you've moved to becoming a full-time PC gamer (if you weren't one already) then PC Game Pass is the way to go.

It is a PC-only tier, and for a little more than what you'd pay for Game Pass Premium, you get:

  • A Game Pass Ultimate-sized library of games to access on your PC
  • Brand-new games on day one, including all Xbox-published games
  • The library of games included in EA Play
  • In-game benefits for select titles
  • Up to $50 a year can be earned through the Microsoft Rewards program

Out of all the tiers, this is easily the best deal since the last Game Pass price hike. It's arguably the last element of Game Pass that you could say harkens back to the idea of the subscription being 'the best deal in gaming,' which also means it'll almost certainly be made worse at some point in the near future.

Xbox Game Pass Games (by Tier)

NOTE: All games listed are included as of March 2026. Xbox Game Pass receives new games every month, and we will continue to update this article as new games enter the service and old ones leave. All titles available on Xbox consoles and PC unless otherwise indicated. The most recent reveal of new Game Pass games will be linked below and updated regularly.

Latest Game Pass Additions (March 17, 2026): Disco Elysium, The Long Dark, Like a Dragon: Infinite Wealth, RE7, Absolum, and More Join Xbox Game Pass

To avoid making this page too hard to scroll through, we'll list only titles exclusive to that subscription tier, so we don't repeat certain games.

So the Game Pass Premium list will include only games not available in Essential, and the Game Pass Ultimate list will include only games not available in Premium. If you pay for Game Pass Premium, you get every game listed in Essential, and if you pay for Game Pass Ultimate, you get every game listed in Essential and Premium.

That's why the Game Pass Ultimate section is not automatically the longest list (that's Game Pass Premium), even though it actually has the most games.

Xbox Game Pass Essential Games (A-Z)

  • Albion Online - (PC Only)
  • Among Us
  • Asphalt Legends
  • Astroneer
  • Batman: Arkham Knight
  • Brawlhalla
  • Call of Duty: Warzone
  • Celeste
  • Chivalry 2
  • Cities: Skylines
  • Control Ultimate Edition
  • Crash Team Racing Nitro-Fueled
  • DayZ
  • Dead Cells
  • Deep Rock Galactic
  • Descenders
  • Dishonored 2
  • Disney Dreamlight Valley

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/xbox-game-pass-games-hub-every-game-available-in-xbox-game-pass/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Take-Two Boss Says GenAI/AI Tools “May Help You Create Assets, But That Won’t Help You Create Hits” Like GTA
item.content:encoded:
Generative AI (GenAI) and the new wave of AI tools that have begun to filter through the video game industry continue to be a controversial topic of conversation in games. The most recent spike in controversy comes from the reveal of NVIDIA's DLSS 5, which was an incredibly impressive reveal for some, but the cause of major backlash that already has some developers part of that initial reveal doing damage control.

While there are a lot of players concerned as to how much GenAI tools will continue to influence how video games look and are developed, Take-Two Interactive chief executive officer Strauss Zelnick once again offered a more measured response to GenAI.

In a new interview with The Game Business, Zelnick offered the same approach that he's been giving since 2023, that GenAI tech and tools are just that, tools, not the one-button-press solution to making big hits that some (like the investors who drove Take-Two, Nintendo, and Roblox's stock down for a day when Google's Project Genie was revealed) seem to think it is.

"The history of our industry is that we've always used technology to create great entertainment. So, an advance in technology that allows us to do things better and quicker is great for us," Zelnick said. "I was kind of stunned by the market's reaction, because its reaction was somehow seeing it as a threat to what we do, when it's quite obvious that creation tools are beneficial for our industry."

"I think the bear case for big entertainment companies is somehow that AI tools will mean everyone can create hits, but that doesn't stand to reason. These tools may help you create assets, but that won't help you create hits. There are loads of assets out there now. It doesn't matter if you push a button to create an asset, or it takes you six weeks, at the end of the day, you have an asset...you can create assets that might look like a big release, that might look like NBA 2K or EA Sports FC. But creating a hit of that magnitude is a completely different animal and does require human engagement and creativity."

Specifically regarding Google's Project Genie (which Google has even admitted isn't a tool it created for making games), when asked if he believed Project Genie or tools like it could allow an individual or smaller entity to make a game like Grand Theft Auto, he responded, "Not even the littlest bit."

"There's already plenty of technology out there that allow people to create video games, and as a result, thousands of video games are created every year, and yet the hits all cluster among the large entertainment companies, almost entirely, and now and then, an indie, which is generally speaking well-funded and pretty robust in and of itself."

"The notion that somehow new tools would allow an individual to push a button and generate a hit and bring it to millions of consumers around the world, it's a laughable notion. It's just never been the case with entertainment. Right now [in music] there are programs that allow you to put out a prompt and get a professionally recorded song spit back out at you. It sounds like a song, but I defy you to listen to it more than once. It's great to send as a greeting card to your partner on their birthday, but that's about it."

These comments are all consistent with what Strauss Zelnick has said before, that GenAI and AI tools on their own could not make games anywhere as good as or on the same scale as GTA. He's also previously made it clear that GenAI tools were not used in the creation of GTA VI.

While there are some high-ranking executives in the industry who seem excited about GenAI tools to such a degree that it seems like they believe their company's ability to make a game as big as GTA VI is just a push of a button away, Strauss Zelnick has been at this for a long time, and his comments serve as yet another reminder that nothing in creative industries works like that. You can't make a hit, or to use Zelnick's music example, anything with actual substance, out of nothing.

No matter what side of the GenAI aisle you land on, whether you think these tools have a place in creative endeavours or not, both sides should be able to agree on that. If you think differently, you're in the realm of "laughable" thinking.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/take-two-boss-strauss-zelnick-genai-may-help-you-create-assets-but-not-hits-like-gta-vi/
